methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.skip_reason: insufficient narrow peer set in Robotics & Manufacturing (found 0 same-stage, 0 adjacent-stage, 0 similar-tier; need ≥3)
methods.rationale: 11 same-sector peers exist in Robotics & Manufacturing, but none of the narrow filters (same-stage / adjacent-stage / similar-valuation-tier) yielded ≥3 datapoints. Falling back to all-sector median would be misleading.

data.narrative:
Lumina Vehicles is a seed-stage robotics and manufacturing company founded by Ahmed Shubber and Federico Di Palma. The valuation analysis draws on a universe of 9 comparable companies—1 public and 8 private—within the Robotics & Manufacturing sector, including peers such as 1X Technologies, Divergent, Exotec, Locus Robotics, and Bedrock Robotics.

The peer-median method, the primary triangulation approach, is not applicable to this valuation. The analysis identified 11 same-sector peers overall, but the filtering criteria for methodological rigor—same stage, adjacent stage, or similar valuation tier—yielded fewer than three datapoints in each category. Using an unfiltered sector median would introduce material selection bias given the wide dispersion in maturity and scale across the robotics landscape. Consequently, no defensible quantitative range can be derived from comparable company analysis at this time.

The absence of sufficient narrow-band comparables reflects both the stage (seed) and the specificity of the sector. Without triangulation results, a valuation range cannot be supported by this analysis. Key uncertainties include the identification of additional seed-stage robotics companies and the degree to which public robotics players or later-stage private robotics firms are appropriate benchmarks for a pre-revenue or early-traction seed entity. A re-assessment should be conducted once additional comparable data or financial performance metrics become available.
